marketState: neutral, tilting bullish near-term on an extreme heat wave, capped longer-term by a growing storage surplus
summary: Front (Aug) is $2.940, essentially at fair value ($2.917, spread +$0.023 = FAIR), but seasonally cheap: price is -13.7% YTD vs a -3.4% median path (-10.3pp), and the 16y history gives only 31-50% odds of being higher over the next 1-4 weeks with a typical 4-week move of -3.1% (reliability flagged Low). The dominant near-term catalyst is weather: 1-7D demand is +18.3% vs 5y (rangePos 0.98, near the top of its range) and 8-16D demand is +50.1% vs 5y with rangePos 1.42 - above the historical max - while the 24h demand trend is +25 and running +36% above normal, an unusually strong and still-building heat signal. Working against that, storage printed a bearish surprise (+61 Bcf build vs -11 Bcf forecast, +72 Bcf surprise) and sits +184 Bcf above the 5y average, and managed money is net short (-60,295, 35th percentile) though not at an extreme. Technicals are bearish-trending from 15m through 1w, but 4h RSI (24.9) and the 1d close below its lower Bollinger band point to a stretched, potentially bounce-prone tape, and the futures curve shows only slight front-to-Sep backwardation (-$0.045) before building into a normal winter contango (Dec $3.788, Jan $4.233). The fair-value curve itself is rising into peak summer (now $2.917 -> +21D $3.127) while spot is flat, so if the heat materializes into real storage draws, price has room to catch up to that rising band; if it doesn't, the seasonal/storage bearish case reasserts.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality (highest-priority signal) is bearish-leaning but explicitly Low reliability (probHigher only 31-50% over 1-4 weeks, typical 4wk move -3.1%), and storage is running a growing +184 Bcf surplus with a bearish build surprise - both argue against chasing longs. But the 8-16D weather signal is the strongest reading in the whole dataset (rangePos 1.42, above the historical max), which could flip the storage trajectory bullish within weeks. The setup is genuinely two-sided and not clean enough for a sized directional bet yet.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either: a storage report that draws tighter than forecast (confirming the heat is actually eating the surplus), or a pullback toward the $2.60-2.75 zone (near the lower fair-value band) to improve entry risk, or the managed-money COT net position stabilizing/turning up from its current 35th percentile.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term price sits right at fair value ($2.917, spread +$0.023 = FAIR) while both weather windows show extreme, still-rising cooling demand (1-7D +18.3% vs 5y near max, 8-16D +50.1% vs 5y above max) - a setup where prompt demand can outrun a flat spot price quickly. 4h RSI (24.9) and the 1d close under its lower Bollinger band suggest the recent bearish-trend slide is stretched and due a bounce, and the fair-value curve is already pricing a climb to $2.95-$3.00 over the next 7-14 days.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close below $2.80 (loss of the fair-value floor and confirmation the heat isn't translating to tighter balances), or the 8-16D demand reading rolling back toward/below 5y normal.
